pattern: Because the graph is acyclic, a depth-first walk that extends the current path along each outgoing edge and records it on reaching the sink enumerates every source-to-sink route without revisiting concerns.
hints: There are no cycles, so you never need a visited set; you can safely follow every outgoing edge as deep as it goes.
Carry the path built so far; on reaching the final node record a copy of it, otherwise recurse into each neighbor.
Depth-first from node 0 appending the current node to the path; when the path's last node is n-1 add it to the results, then backtrack to try other branches.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct paths to the sink
branching graph with many paths of different lengths
minimum two-node graph with a single direct edge
linear chain has exactly one path
source connects directly and indirectly to the sink

stress.description: random DAGs over a topological numbering with guaranteed reachability of the sink
stress.note: lower-to-higher edges keep it a DAG; node 0 to n-1 always has at least one path

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def allPathsSourceTarget(self, graph):
        n = len(graph)
        target = n - 1
        from collections import deque
        results = []
        stack = [[0]]
        while stack:
            path = stack.pop()
            node = path[-1]
            if node == target:
                results.append(path)
                continue
            for nb in graph[node]:
                stack.append(path + [nb])
        results.sort()
        return results

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def allPathsSourceTarget(self, graph):
        n = len(graph)
        target = n - 1
        res = []
        path = [0]
        def dfs(node):
            if node == target:
                res.append(path[:])
                return
            for nb in graph[node]:
                path.append(nb)
                dfs(nb)
                path.pop()
        dfs(0)
        res.sort()
        return res
